comparison.title: CPU Curated vs Traditional College Experience
comparison.subtitle: Why is CPU Curated experience better than traditional colleges?

rows.others: <25% students receive an internship by their 3rd year

rows.others: 1–2 final-year projects, rarely deployed or production-grade

rows.cpu: Industry-relevant curriculum built and taught by experts from Google & Amazon
rows.others: Rarely updated curriculum, mostly academic-only faculty

rows.cpu: Start coding and build real projects from Day 1
rows.others: Build real world projects only by the end of 3rd–4th year

rows.others: Good startup culture but mostly without funding support

rows.cpu: Frequent sessions with tech leaders, CTOs, VCs and startup founders
rows.others: Industry exposure limited to top students only

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about CPU's AI-First B.Tech before you take the next step.

questions.question: What are the eligibility criteria for admission?
questions.answer: You must have completed Class 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics (PCM) and secured a minimum of 50% marks. Admission is competitive — only the top 20–25% of applicants are selected via CPUEST and a Personal Interview.

questions.question: What is the CPUEST and how do I prepare for it?
questions.answer: CPUEST stands for Career Point Scholastic Aptitude Test. It covers three areas: Mathematics (Class 11–12 level), Physics (Class 11–12 level), Chemistry (Class 11–12 level). The test can be taken online or at the Kota campus. It is scheduled for 28 March 2026. Brush up on your Class 12 Maths, Physics, Chemistry and practice aptitude questions to prepare.

questions.question: What are the key dates for Batch 2026–27?
questions.answer: Applications Open: March 2026, Application Deadline: 27 March 2026, CPUEST Date: 28 March 2026, Personal Interviews: Shortly after CPUEST results, Batch Begins: July 2026. Seats are limited and selection is competitive. Apply early to secure your spot.

questions.question: What is the step-by-step admission process?
questions.answer: Step 1 — Register & Apply Online: Submit your application with 10+2 marksheet and PCM details. Step 2 — Appear for CPUEST: Take the aptitude test online or at campus (Maths, Chemistry, Physics). Step 3 — Personal Interview: Faculty panel interview testing problem-solving ability and passion for technology. Step 4 — Counselling & Seat Confirmation: One-on-one session to submit documents and confirm your seat.

questions.question: Is the B.Tech degree recognised? Will employers accept it?
questions.answer: Yes, absolutely. The B.Tech degree from Career Point University is fully recognised: UGC Recognised — valid for all government and private sector roles, AICTE Approved — the national regulator for technical education, NAAC Accredited — quality-accredited university. It carries the same weight as any other UGC-recognised B.Tech for jobs, higher studies, GATE, and more.

questions.question: When does AI learning begin — and what tools will I use?
questions.answer: AI begins from Semester 1, Day 1 — not Year 4. You'll use 12+ AI tools across the programme, including: Year 1: ChatGPT, Notion AI, Year 2: OpenAI API, Google Gemini API, Hugging Face, LangChain, Year 3: TensorFlow, PyTorch, Stable Diffusion, Year 4: Claude AI, Cursor, Vercel AI SDK. Every tool is used inside real shipped projects — not just mentioned in a lecture.

questions.question: What real projects will I build during the 4 years?
questions.answer: You will ship 15 portfolio-grade projects — all deployed and recruiter-ready. Highlights include: Personal Portfolio Website, E-Commerce Page, AI Quiz App (Year 1), Social Media Platform, AI Chatbot, GenAI-Powered CRM (Year 2), AI Image Generator, Video Streaming Platform, ML Deploy Pipeline (Year 3), AI Code Assistant, Job Portal with AI Resume Match, Industry Capstone (Year 4). You graduate with an active GitHub portfolio of 15 live, deployed applications.

questions.question: Who teaches the courses? Are they working professionals?
questions.answer: The curriculum is built and taught by industry practitioners from companies like Google, Amazon, and Microsoft — not just academics with textbooks. You'll also have weekly sessions from MAANG engineers, CTOs, VCs, and startup founders throughout the programme.

questions.question: What is the structure of the 4-year programme?
questions.answer: Each year has a clear theme with 29 subjects across 8 semesters: Year 1 — Build: C++, HTML/CSS/JS, React, DSA, Discrete Maths, Year 2 — Develop: Node.js, MongoDB, Java, AI Engineering with LLMs, APIs, Year 3 — Specialize: ML, NLP, Computer Vision, AWS, System Design + 6-month Internship, Year 4 — Launch: AI Product Development, Placement Bootcamp, Industry Capstone.

questions.question: What is the internship and placement track record?
questions.answer: 93% of 2nd year students secured paid internships, Average internship stipend: ₹25,000/month, 6-month formal internship in Year 3 with a partner company, 1000+ hiring partner companies in the network. Compare this to Tier 1 colleges where fewer than 50% of students receive an internship by their 3rd year.

questions.question: When does career preparation actually start?
questions.answer: Career prep starts from Week 1 — not Year 4: LeetCode & GitHub profiles set up at orientation, Weekly coding sessions via AlgoSphere club from Semester 1, MAANG engineers deliver weekly sessions in Year 2, Intensive DSA bootcamp + 6-month internship in Year 3, Mock drives, system design prep, company-specific coaching in Year 4.

questions.question: Is there support for entrepreneurship and startups?
questions.answer: Yes. CPU has a strong entrepreneurship ecosystem: ₹1 Crore StartX Fund for student startups, Expert mentors, VCs, and startup founders on campus regularly, Participation supported in GSOC, ICPC, SIH hackathons, The AI Foundry — innovation arena for demo days and ideation, Idea Lab entrepreneurship club for like-minded builders.

questions.question: What certifications can I earn alongside the degree?
questions.answer: Students are guided to earn globally recognised certifications during Year 3–4, including: AWS / Google Cloud certifications, DeepLearning.AI specialisations, MLOps and DevOps credentials. These complement the B.Tech degree and significantly strengthen your resume for MAANG-level roles.

questions.question: What scholarships are available and how do I get them?
questions.answer: CPU offers merit-based scholarships to make quality education accessible: Merit Excellence Scholarship — Up to 100% tuition (based on CPUEST score & interview), Bright Minds Scholarship — Up to 50% tuition (based on Class 12 marks or JEE Mains score), Women in Tech Scholarship — Up to 30% tuition (high-potential female students), CP Alumni Benefit — Special fee waiver for wards & siblings of CP alumni. Perform well in CPUEST to maximise your scholarship eligibility. The better you score, the higher the scholarship.

questions.question: Are education loans available? Which banks support CPU?
questions.answer: Yes. CPU has official finance partnerships with leading institutions: Axis Bank — up to 100% loan coverage, IDFC First Bank — flexible repayment options, Propelld — education-focused fintech loans. Our admission counsellors can guide you through the loan process during the counselling session.

questions.question: Can I pay fees in instalments?
questions.answer: Yes. Fees are structured semester-wise, so you pay in instalments over 8 semesters rather than the full amount upfront. Speak to our admission counsellor for the exact fee schedule and instalment plan — contact us at info@cpuniverse.in or call +91-9079134713.

questions.question: Is hostel accommodation available on campus?
questions.answer: Yes. Campus accommodation at CP Tower, Kota includes: AC rooms with high-speed Wi-Fi, Quality food (mess facility), 24/7 power backup, CCTV security throughout, On-campus medical facility.

questions.question: What tech infrastructure is available for students?
questions.answer: CPU is built for builders: 24/7 AI Coding Hub — open round the clock at CP Tower to build, debug and ship at any hour, AI Smart Classrooms — high-speed Wi-Fi, digital screens, state-of-the-art AV systems, The AI Foundry — innovation arena for hackathons, demo days and collaboration.

questions.question: Are there student clubs and extracurricular activities?
questions.answer: Campus life is vibrant with 5 active student clubs: AlgoSphere — Weekly coding sessions & competitive programming, HackTheWeb — AI & Web Dev hackathons, Idea Lab — Entrepreneurship, startup ideation, StageLab — Cultural events & performing arts, StriveX — Sports & fitness.

questions.question: Where is the campus located and why Kota?
questions.answer: The campus is at CP Tower, Kota, Rajasthan 324005. Kota is India's education capital — known for producing top engineers and IITians for over 30 years. Career Point has been at the heart of Kota's education ecosystem since 1993, giving you access to a deeply competitive, high-performance academic environment.

footer.description: India's first AI-powered B.Tech program designed for the next generation of tech leaders. Build real projects from day one and launch your career in tech.
